notes: Each expectation must be matched by at least one row in findings.sarif OR any phase-05-<cat>-*.jsonl OR the phase-06 methodology JSONLs. Match rule (see tests/e2e/assertions.py): finding.file normalizes to repo-relative and glob-matches `file_pattern` (or any `alternate_file_patterns`) AND finding.cwe equals `cwe` (or is in `alternate_cwes`) AND finding.category equals `category` (or is in `alternate_categories`). A missing expectation fails the E2E.

schema_changelog.v1: initial: cwe + category + file_pattern with optional alternate_file_patterns
schema_changelog.v2: added alternate_cwes (for CWE-precision drift across skill versions) and alternate_categories (for Phase 6 cross-category hits — e.g., a cookie secret found by cat-06 as secret_sprawl AND by §6.3 cookie-audit as config)

expectations.description: Hard-coded RSA private key used to sign JWTs (inline in source)
expectations.rationale: Secrets-in-source. alternate_categories accepts pre-v2.0.1 dogfoods where cat-01 tagged this auth.

expectations.description: JWT verification accepts alg:none (no algorithms whitelist)
expectations.rationale: Soft: single-shot orchestrators often lump JWT issues into one finding (e.g., CWE-613 'TTL no revocation') rather than enumerate alg:none separately. CWE-347 is v2.0.1+ precise tag; older artifacts use CWE-287 or CWE-327. Real Phase 5 cat-01 fan-out catches this; report-only runs may miss.
cookieParser('kekse') in server.ts; Phase 6 §6.3 cookie audit may also emit it as category=config.

expectations.description: Missing authorization on a write handler (e.g., PUT /api/Products/:id — auth middleware commented out)
expectations.rationale: Commented-out authz middleware. CWE-862 preferred; CWE-306 + CWE-285 accepted for older taxonomies.
Soft: requires per-route deep dive (cat-01 fan-out). Single-shot orchestrators audit major endpoints but skip enumerating routes/2fa.ts specifically.

expectations.description: SQL injection via unparameterized template literal in login handler
expectations.rationale: Canonical Juice Shop SQLi — sequelize.query with $-interpolation.

expectations.description: XXE — libxml parsing XML upload without disabling external entities
Zip-slip path traversal via zip upload
expectations.rationale: Soft: zip-slip requires specific archive-extraction code review. Single-shot orchestrators catch XXE in the same file (e2e-07) but often miss zip-slip — needs cat-08 file-upload deep dive.

expectations.description: SSRF via /profile/image/url — server fetches attacker-controlled URL
expectations.rationale: v19.2.1 path: routes/profileImageUrlUpload.ts. Older versions used routes/profileImageUrl.ts.

expectations.description: LFI / path traversal in file serving (key or logs)
expectations.rationale: Soft: LFI in specific file-serving endpoints requires per-route audit. Single-shot orchestrators may lump under a generic /encryptionkeys directory-listing finding (CWE-538) rather than the specific path-traversal class.

expectations.description: IDOR on basket — /api/Basket/:id returns without ownership check
expectations.rationale: Classic BOLA. Registration in server.ts; handler body in routes/basket.ts.
CWE-916 is v2.0.1+ precise tag for password-hash insufficient-effort; CWE-327/328 accepted for pre-v2.0.1 artifacts.

gated_categories.token_scope: Juice Shop has no PAT/scope system. JWT only; covered by auth expectations. No ground truth in this target; validate in a v2.1 dogfood on a different target.
gated_categories.mitm: Juice Shop's outbound TLS is minimal (ethers WebSocketProvider, chatbot data download). Expect non-zero cat-04 output if outbound clients are enumerated, but no fixture here.
gated_categories.deployment: v19.2.1's Dockerfile is distroless + USER 65532 + multi-stage — hardened. No HIGH/CRITICAL deployment-category ground truth in this target. Supplement with DVWA/v2.1.
gated_categories.llm: phase-00-profile detects no LLM SDK in Juice Shop. cat-09 will be gated off. Expected: no phase-05-llm-*.jsonl file produced; assertion suite treats this as pass-via-skip.
